name: adaptive-chemotaxis
description: Study 3 — adaptive chemotaxis: where life becomes mind. A population of run-and-tumble agents in a nutrient gradient senses the local food and tumbles less when nutrient is rising (bacterial chemotaxis); nutrient sustains the viability that keeps each agent alive. A chemotactic agent climbs the gradient, finds food, and survives; a blind agent (``chemotactic=false``) random-walks, starves, and dissolves. The gradient is MEANINGFUL from the perspective of the precarious identity — sense-making, agency in service of self-maintenance.

chemotactic.description: Sense the gradient and bias tumbling up it. false = the blind-motile control.

seed.description: RNG seed for the agent ensemble + run-and-tumble draws (deterministic per seed).
